chapter_context.summary: Prophesies Zedekiah's captivity and peaceful death, and fiercely denounces the wealthy citizens who greedily re-enslaved their freed servants.

outline.text: Message to Zedekiah of Captivity and Peaceful Burial
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Message to Zedekiah of Captivity and Peaceful Burial (34:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Covenant of Liberty Made and Violated by Judah's Nobles
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Covenant of Liberty Made and Violated by Judah's Nobles (34:8-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Proclaiming Liberty to Sword and Pestilence for Nobles
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Proclaiming Liberty to Sword and Pestilence for Nobles (34:17-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 34, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
